name: Chartres Cathedral
description: Chartres Cathedral, a masterpiece of Gothic architecture, holds immense significance in French mythology and tradition. Built upon the site of a pre-Christian sacred well and a Roman temple, it is believed to be a powerful telluric center. Legend claims that the cathedral houses a 'Virgo paritura,' a sacred image of a mother goddess, predating Christianity. The labyrinth on the cathedral floor is seen as a symbolic pilgrimage to Jerusalem, a journey of spiritual transformation. The stained glass windows, particularly the famous 'Chartres Blue,' are believed to possess mystical properties, capable of healing and inspiring divine visions. The presence of the Virgin Mary, deeply revered in French Catholicism, is strongly felt within the cathedral, making it a place of pilgrimage and miracles. Some believe the cathedral is built on ley lines, connecting it to other sacred sites across Europe, amplifying its spiritual power. Its association with the Knights Templar and the Rose Cross further adds to its mystique, with whispers of hidden knowledge and esoteric practices connected to the site. The cathedral represents a convergence of pagan, Christian, and esoteric beliefs, making it a potent symbol of French spiritual heritage, and a place where the veil between the earthly and divine realms is thin. The deep well beneath the cathedral, historically used for pagan rituals, adds a layer of ancient mystery and power to the site. It continues to be a place of wonder, inspiring artists, writers, and spiritual seekers from around the world. Pilgrims still flock to Chartres seeking healing, guidance, and a connection to the sacred.
subtitle: A Gothic masterpiece, a spiritual power center.

keyMyths.title: The Sacred Well
keyMyths.description: Beneath the cathedral lies a sacred well, believed to have been a site of pre-Christian worship. It is said to hold powerful energies and connect to the earth's life force.

keyMyths.title: The Labyrinth's Journey
keyMyths.description: The labyrinth on the floor represents a symbolic pilgrimage to Jerusalem, a journey of spiritual cleansing and transformation. Walking the labyrinth is a meditative practice.

sources.author: Jan van der Cruysse
sources.description: Explores the geometric principles and sacred significance of Chartres Cathedral.
